title: Fix standalone plan to sprint contract materialization
description: The standalone plan command writes only .bober/specs/*.json; the spec-to-contracts materialization lives solely inside the run pipeline, so sprint errors with 'No sprint contracts found' even though plan prints 'Next: sprint'. This plan extracts a single shared materializeContracts helper used by both plan and run, has plan eagerly materialize contracts (preferring the planner's embedded spec.sprints with feature-derived fallback), gives materialized contracts deterministic zero-padded ids, and hardens the sprint command against the failure modes eager materialization amplifies.

resolvedClarifications.answer: approve . Changing run's research/plan/contract behavior (frozen) — including making run honor spec.sprints[]. this would make sense. 3. Numeric/topological ordering for >=10 sprints (C7, pre-existing) beyond trivial zero-padding. this as well
Eager at end of plan — plan writes contracts immediately after the spec.
Prefer valid spec.sprints, else regenerate from features via generateContractPrecision.

Extract a shared materializeContracts helper used by both plan and the run pipeline. Owner additionally authorized run to honor embedded spec.sprints (lifting the freeze) so the helper needs no mode flag.

assumptions: The standalone plan-then-sprint flow is an intended, supported path (plan.ts:171 prints 'Next: npx agent-bober sprint').
spec.sprints[] is incidental, unvalidated model passthrough (spec.ts:160 z.array(z.unknown()); runPlanner only saveSpec-s at planner-agent.ts:275), so every entry must be safeParse-d, never trusted.
Materialized contracts are forced to status 'proposed' regardless of any embedded status, so findNextPendingSprint (sprint.ts:38-43) picks them up.
Eager materialization runs only when the spec is not needs-clarification; a parked spec writes zero contracts.
Owner authorized lifting the run-pipeline freeze: run and plan share one helper and run now honors valid embedded spec.sprints, skipping per-feature generateContractPrecision LLM calls when present.
The protected invariant is that feature-derived contract content is preserved when no embedded sprints exist; contract ids legitimately change to deterministic zero-padded sprint-<specId>-NN form.
A build is required for the CLI to pick up the fix: npm run build (and npm run update-all for skill copies) is part of done.
Re-running plan for the same specId must overwrite that specId's prior contracts without leaving stale files behind.
outOfScope: A new standalone contracts CLI command (rejected design fork).
Topological reordering of sprints by dependsOn (only lexical ordering via zero-padded ids is addressed).
Broad rework of the clarification gate beyond the minimal sprint-side guard in Sprint 3.
Provider-specific planner-prompt changes to coerce schema-perfect spec.sprints[].
Multi-spec parallel execution semantics beyond scoping the sprint command to the latest spec.

features.title: Shared deterministic materializeContracts helper
features.description: Extract the pipeline contract-creation loop into a reusable materializeContracts helper that both the run pipeline and the plan command call, producing deterministic zero-padded contract ids while preserving feature-derived contract content.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When npm run build runs after the refactor, it completes with zero TypeScript errors.

AC2: When runTsPipeline creates contracts, it delegates to materializeContracts and the feature-derived contract content matches the pre-refactor output for a multi-feature spec with no embedded sprints.
AC3: When a spec has twelve or more sprints, the materialized contract ids are zero-padded so lexical ordering equals execution order.

features.title: Embedded-sprint materialization and eager wiring into plan
features.description: Teach materializeContracts to prefer valid embedded spec.sprints with feature-derived fallback, and wire the plan command to eagerly materialize contracts after a successful non-clarification plan.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When plan completes for a ready spec, the contracts directory contains one schema-valid contract per sprint.
AC2: When spec.sprints entries are valid they are used with status normalized to proposed; when invalid, feature-derived generation is used without throwing.
AC3: When a spec is needs-clarification, plan writes zero contracts.
AC4: When plan is re-run for the same specId, prior contracts for that specId are overwritten without stale leftovers.

features.title: Scope sprint to the active spec with clarification guard
features.description: Make the sprint command run only the contracts belonging to the latest spec, refuse a needs-clarification spec with a clear message, and improve the empty-contracts message.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When contracts from multiple specs are present, sprint runs only the active spec's contracts.
AC2: When the active spec is needs-clarification, sprint refuses with a message naming the open clarifications and invokes no generator.
AC3: When no contracts match the active spec, the error message instructs the user to re-plan or run the full pipeline.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Contract materialization has a single source of truth shared by plan and the run pipeline.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Code review confirms one materializeContracts helper imported by both src/cli/commands/plan.ts and src/orchestrator/pipeline.ts.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Materialization never throws on malformed spec.sprints input.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: A unit test passing malformed spec.sprints entries asserts no throw and that valid feature-derived contracts are written instead.

techNotes.dataModel: SprintContract (src/contracts/sprint-contract.ts) and PlanSpec (src/contracts/spec.ts); contracts persisted under .bober/contracts via saveContract (src/state/sprint-state.ts).
securityConsiderations: No new external input surface; spec.sprints is parsed via SprintContractSchema.safeParse before any disk write.
techNotes.existingPatterns: Follow the contract-creation loop at pipeline.ts:856-906 and the save/list helpers in src/state/sprint-state.ts.

metadata.riskNotes: Refactors the previously-frozen run pipeline; mitigated by characterization tests asserting feature-derived content parity and deterministic ids. Owner explicitly authorized the run behavior change so run now honors embedded spec.sprints.
